title: Tilray Brands Positioned for U.S. Expansion Amid Historic Cannabis Rescheduling
text: Tilray Brands is positioning itself to capitalize on cannabis rescheduling from Schedule I to Schedule III under the Controlled Substances Act, which CEO Irwin Simon called a pivotal moment for the United States. The company plans to leverage its global medical cannabis infrastructure—including 7 million square feet of cultivation capacity and experience serving hundreds of thousands of patients across 20+ countries—to expand U.S. medical access. Tilray Medical is actively evaluating participation in the Center for Medicare and Medicaid Innovation (CMMI) pilot program, which would position the company as a supplier to healthcare organizations delivering hemp-derived medical cannabis to underserved patient populations while generating clinical outcomes data. The company has pharmaceutical-quality cultivation and manufacturing infrastructure, regulatory expertise from operating in highly regulated international markets, and an established portfolio of cannabinoid formulations across multiple product formats.
